title: DRSW/1 workspace payload (kind drc-workspace, v 1)
description: The plaintext JSON a Deep Research Secure Workspace envelope seals — the interchange bundle a conforming node opens and applies. Normative prose: docs/WORKSPACE-PROTOCOL.md. Readers MUST ignore members not described here (forward compatibility); writers MUST omit empty sections rather than emitting them empty.

kind.description: Payload type discriminator (the registered baseline kind).

name.description: Display name for the opened workspace.

note.description: Welcome note shown on open, before anything is applied.

keys.description: Provider API credentials, keyed by provider id (e.g. openai, groq, berget). The most sensitive section — writers MUST warn before including it; readers SHOULD stage its application behind explicit consent.

providerId.description: The active provider choice accompanying keys.

model.description: The active model choice accompanying keys.

settings.description: Behavior knobs. All optional; unknown members ignored.

searchBackend.description: Self-hosted web-search backend config (may carry a service key).

localBaseUrl.description: The user's own OpenAI-compatible server base URL (the keyless local provider).

conversations.description: Portable chat state. Readers MUST append these as new conversations (fresh ids) — never clobber local data.

grants.description: Borrowed metered capabilities — signed tokens whose allowance lives in the ISSUING node's meter, administered live by the minter. Only URL-safe token tiers may travel (never post-exchange working tokens). Hydration is opportunistic and fail-soft: the workspace opens fully offline regardless.

ws.description: deepresearch.se shorthand: a wsk1.… web-search grant token.

proxy.description: deepresearch.se shorthand: prg1.… proxy GRANT tokens (the token-granting tier), one per service.

tokens.description: Interop extension: issuer-qualified grant entries, so one workspace can carry allowances from several nodes.

issuer.description: Origin of the node whose meter backs this token.

type.description: Grant family/type at the issuer (e.g. web, api, server-token).

origin.description: Interop extension: provenance of the exporting node (private — inside the ciphertext).

software.description: Implementation identifier, e.g. a repository URL.

pipelines.description: Interop extension: DRPL/1 pipeline declarations relevant to this workspace's research (see docs/schemas/drpl-1.schema.json) — carried so any node can show, compare, or re-run the declared structure.

provenance.description: Interop extension: the processing/enrichment trail — one entry per node hop that transformed this workspace. Append-only by convention; entirely user-controlled and private (inside the ciphertext).

at.description: Unix ms timestamp of the hop.

pipeline.description: The DRPL document id that ran.

fp.description: The DRPL structural fingerprint that ran (drpl1:<level>:<hex>).

route.description: Interop extension: a planned itinerary across nodes — the navigation/routing primitive. The user's client advances the cursor and re-seals per hop; no server ever sees it.

cursor.description: Index of the next hop to take.

portal.description: Portal path at that node if it differs from its discovery manifest.

pipeline.description: The DRPL document id intended to run there.
